characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: He sometimes gets referred to as "Olie Olie" by Zowie. Be Careful What You Wish For: "1 Olie, 2 Olie, 3 Olie, 4" has him duplicating himself to try and make the duplicate do his homework for him. Unfortunately, the duplicate ends up doing the same thing,and it eventually gets to the point where there are99Olies in the house. Big Brother Mentor: Should Zowie ever ask him how something he knows works, Olie will gladly show her. Catchphrase: He tends to say "Neat-o!" whenever he gets the hang of something. Cheerful Child: Olie is a young boy who is one of the most cheery and positive people you will ever run into.

characters.name: Olie
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: He sometimes gets referred to as "Olie Olie" by Zowie. Be Careful What You Wish For: "1 Olie, 2 Olie, 3 Olie, 4" has him duplicating himself to try and make the duplicate do his homework for him. Unfortunately, the duplicate ends up doing the same thing,and it eventually gets to the point where there are99Olies in the house. Big Brother Mentor: Should Zowie ever ask him how something he knows works, Olie will gladly show her. Catchphrase: He tends to say "Neat-o!" whenever he gets the hang of something. Cheerful Child: Olie is a young boy who is one of the most cheery and positive people you will ever run into.

characters.name: Zowie
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: She frequently gets called "Zo" by the rest of her family. Annoying Younger Sibling: As Olie puts it, she can sometimes be a "pain in the gears", but most of the time it's out of inquisitiveness. In "Copy Cat-n-Mouse", though, she intentionally acts uncooperative towards Olie so that she won't go inside and have a nap. Baby See, Baby Do: She naively copies her father's actions in "Dingliedangliedoodle" and even repeats said word without knowing that it's an in-universe cuss word. Be Careful What You Wish For: In "Polie Pox", she becomes jealous of Olie having ice cream in bed and vows to have the same thing happen to her.It does, but only through her getting sick as well. Big Brother Worship: She adores Olie and frequently asks him about things she doesn't understand.

characters.name: Mr. Percy Polie
characters.description: Bungling Inventor: His inventions sometimes didn't work. Demoted to Extra: He is absent in some of the episodes and has a minor role in a few episodes.

characters.name: Mrs. Polina Polie
characters.description: Ascended Extra: She becomes a major character in theGrand FinaleThe Baby Bot Chaseafter beingDemoted to Extrain "The Great Defender of Fun". Demoted to Extra: In "The Great Defender of Fun", where she has brief appearances throughout the movie. Her only main scene is when she rescues Zowie and Pappy with Percy. Girly Girl: She is beautiful and upbeat. Out of Focus: She is a supporting character in "The Great Defender of Fun".

characters.name: Spot
characters.description: Ascended Extra: He is a recurring character in the series, but he is a major character in "The Great Defender of Fun". Big Friendly Dog:Invertedwith his size, but he's still a very affectionate and loyal dog to his owners. Played straight on more than one occasion — in "Gone Dog", he gets zapped by the Magnotron and grows a rather large size, and in "Diary Daze", Olie writes a fictional story about him where he's a giant dog who's covered in spots. Comically Missing the Point: Sometimes does some things which are not what people tell him to do, particularly in "Through Thick and Thin" when Olie is trying to get him to be Space Dog in his "Space Boy and Space Dog" game. Demoted to Extra: Spot becomes a minor character in "The Baby Bot Chase". He has a lot of rare cameo appearances throughout the movie. It's very easy to forget he is in this movie. Dogs Are Dumb: Averted most of the time as he does prove to be capable of doing what he has been assigned to do, but he sometimes has his less intelligent moments. See the above trope to get the idea.

characters.name: Pappy Polie
characters.description: Cloudcuckoolander: He is sometimes crazy and loses his teeth. Demoted to Extra: In Season 2, where he appearsin one of the episodes' three segments. He returns as anAscended Extracharacter from Season 3 to present. False Teeth Tomfoolery: His "chompers" are a regular source of trouble, popping out of his mouth and eating everything in sight. Out of Focus: In Coochie and Coo's story arc, he only appears in "Kooky Kites" and is absent in "The Baby Bot Chase". Tritagonist: Of "The Great Defender of Fun", where Olie isThe Protagonistand Zowie and Billy are theDeuteragonists.

characters.name: Uncle Gizmo
characters.description: Ascended Extra:He is a cameo character in the first season, debuting in "Our Two Dads" in a major role, and a minor role in Season 2, only appearing in both "A Little Souped Coup-y" and "Beach Blanket Gizmo" asthe main protagonist. Starting the third season, he becomes a recurring character.He becomes a major character in "The Great Defender of Fun". He is a cameo character in the first season, debuting in "Our Two Dads" in a major role, and a minor role in Season 2, only appearing in both "A Little Souped Coup-y" and "Beach Blanket Gizmo" asthe main protagonist. Starting the third season, he becomes a recurring character. He becomes a major character in "The Great Defender of Fun". Butt-Monkey: In "The Baby Bot Chase" where he gets the float finished in time for the parade. Demoted to Extra: Despite his name appearing in the opening credits ofThe Baby Bot Chase, he has a few brief appearances.

characters.name: Billy
characters.description: Affection-Hating Kid: He doesn't like hugs. Ascended Extra: He is a recurring character in the first two seasons. He has a larger role in the last four seasons. Demoted to Extra: After being theDeuteragonistof "The Great Defender of Fun", he has a few brief cameo appearances in "The Baby Bot Chase". Out of Focus: He has a few small speaking lines in "The Baby Bot Chase". Token Minority: He, his family and Chuck Squarey are the only square residents of the Polie Planet.

characters.name: Binky
characters.description: Baby See, Baby Do:At the end of "Dingliedangliedoodle", much like how Zowie imitated Mr. Polie saying the in-universe cuss word, he imitates Mr. Bevel's utterance of the word after the latter falls over while trying to box a punching bag. Baby's First Words:He is able to say Zowie's name at the end of "Baby Binky", his debut episode, no less. Baby Talk: Being easily the youngest character in the main cast, gibberish is really the only way he can communicate. In the episode "Baby Talk", Olie and Billy decide that his gibberish is a language in its own right, and by the end, both are self-proclaimed experts on it. Cloudcuckoolander: Is very much lost in a world of his own with little sense of what happens around him. Fearless Fool:In "Spot That Hero", he walks onto the street which is currently very busy, and it's up to Spot totake a level in badassand earn everyone's approval by saving him from being run over.

characters.name: Dicey
characters.description: Cats Are Mean: Played with. Dicey is not mean per se, but she can be distant and has a friendly rivalry with Spot. Female Feline, Male Mutt: The female feline to Spot's male mutt. Vitriolic Best Buds: Mostly one sided on Spot's end, but she has a friendly rivalry with Spot and still teases him from time to time. Her first appearance has her act rocky and rough with Spot. But she has since mellowed out.

characters.name: Pollie Pi
characters.description: Improbable Hair Style: Has a short bob with 2 buns tied and stacked upright on the top. (which her antenna sticks through) Nice Girl: One of the friendliest female inhabitants of the Polie Planet. Platonic Life-Partners: She seems to have feelings for Olie.

characters.name: Space Boy and Space Dog
characters.description: Hero of Another Story: He is the protagonist of a realityShow Within a Showcalled ''The Adventures of Space Boy." Kid Hero: He is the star of his own TV series and often helps Olie on occasion. Palette Swap: They are basically blue and silver versions of Olie and Spot.

characters.name: Screwy
characters.description: The Bully: He spends most of his screentime antagonizing Olie and his friends for petty reasons. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He may a bully, but he is still kind enough to attend Zowie's birthday party inThe Great Defender of Fun. Vocal Evolution: Starting in season 6, his voice gets noticeably deeper. This is possibly due to Noah Reid hitting puberty.

characters.name: Big Jean and Little Jean Little Green
characters.description: Two aliens from the Little Green Planet whom Olie and his dad meet after their ship crash-landed on Planet Polie. Little Jean eventually forms a firm friendship with Olie. Affectionate Nickname: Little Jean gets called Junior by almost everyone. A Day in the Limelight: "Invasion of the Ticklers" has an interesting variation on the trope in that it's not necessarily them who have the central focus, but rather theirpets, who have made their way to the Polie planet and can't get themselves to go back home with their owners. However, their inclusion in the episode still gives us a bit of a look into the Little Greens' background. Early Installment Character-Design Difference: In their introductory episode, they have a setting on their belly buttons which can make them speak in either their native Little Green Planet language ora really strange, jumbled form of English a la Yoda. These are noticeably not present in any of their appearances after that. Interspecies Friendship: Olie and Little Jean develop a rather solid friendship despite not seeing each other very often and being of a completely different species.

characters.name: Chunk Squarey
characters.description: No Celebrities Were Harmed: Chunk Squarey, including his name, personality, and appearance, is loosely based on Chuck Berry.

characters.name: Klanky Klaus
characters.description: Pseudo-Santa: He's the show's equivalent to Santa Claus, again. Name, appearance, and whole shtick; you get the idea. He also lives in Chillsville, the universe's equivalent to the North Pole.

characters.name: Gloomius Maximus
characters.description: Because You Were Nice to Me: It's because of Zowie's compassion that he eventually manages to pull off hisHeel–Face Turn. Benevolent Boss: He treats his henchmen like family. Berserk Button: InThe Great Defender of Fun, he goes nuts whenever other robots laugh at him. He even reaches a boiling point in "Give It Back, Gloomius" when one of the baddies laughs at his misfortune. Big Bad: OfThe Great Defender of Fun. Blade Below the Shoulder: Instead of aHook Handlike any other pirate would have, he instead has a rapier-like sword attached to his hand. He rarely uses it in combat though.

characters.name: The Baddies
characters.description: My Master, Right or Wrong: They serve Gloomius both before and after hisHeel–Face Turn.
